master_row.title: Environment-Induced Superselection
master_row.canonical_statement: Certain sets of states (pointer states) are stable against decoherence because the environment causes rapid decoherence of superpositions between them; mathematically, density matrices evolve to block-diagonal form in the basis of these pointer states within finite time.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an expectation / trace over state relating ρ, p_i, j.
master_row.dictionary: Certain sets of states (pointer states) are stable against decoherence because the environment causes rapid decoherence of superpositions between them; mathematically, density matrices evolve to block‑diagonal form in the basis of these pointer states within finite time.
master_row.encyclopedia: Entropy measures quantum correlation between subsystems. Interpretive role: This entry refines decoherence into basis selection. It explains why some states are persistent records while other superpositions rapidly disappear from effective observation.
